title: Publisher Confirms, Windows, Failure Ambiguity, and Republish · Stakeholder TypeScript Mission
summary: A privacy-sensitive case-management platform. They need durable and ephemeral subscribers, fanout and topic routing, strict event envelopes, compatible schemas, bounded publisher buffers, unroutable detection, confirm windows, and ambiguous-publication recovery. Planned artifact: Deliver Versioned Multi-Subscriber Event Backbone with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy.
Enumerate overlapping topic bindings and prove which queues receive one copy even when multiple bindings on the same queue match.
Test producer and consumer compatibility for additive, optional, renamed, split, semantic, and removal changes across an overlap window.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility
steps.instruction:
project case — A project messaging team handles case 9986 in a consumer fleet under a shared dependency outage. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound the confirm window and preserve ambiguity when a channel closes before proof; show recovery after the changed failure is removed; then raise a broker resource alarm and stop new publisher admission. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility.
